skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the inference.sh CLI. The skill supports text prompts, image edits, multiple outputs, aspect ratios, high resolution, and Google Search grounding.
skill.summary: Create Gemini image generations and edits through the inference.sh command line workflow.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command examples and hardcoded URLs. Review confirmed that command execution and network access are core to the documented inference.sh image workflow, but they still create medium operational risk. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are false positives from ordinary words such as description and design, with no evidence of cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run the inference.sh CLI through allowed Bash commands. This is legitimate for the stated image generation purpose, but user prompts and image references are passed to an external command and service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static command findings map to documented CLI usage and the allowed-tools declaration. The risk is real because the skill invokes an external command, but the commands match the stated function.

medium_findings.title: External Image Service And URL Access
medium_findings.description: The skill relies on inference.sh and Google-backed image generation, and one example accepts an external image URL. This creates expected network exposure for prompts, image inputs, and generated outputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are visible and consistent with the service documentation and examples. No hidden exfiltration endpoint was found, but network transfer is inherent to the workflow.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography matches occur on documentation words and table labels, not cryptographic implementations. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter, design text, or table descriptions. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, key handling, or cipher usage in those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Pattern For Remote Inference
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples repeatedly invoke infsh app run and npx skills add. These command patterns are expected, but marketplace users should understand that prompts and image inputs leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in command examples. The commands are not malicious on their face, but they execute local tooling and contact remote services.

content.user_title: Generate Gemini Images with Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Image creation workflows often need repeatable prompts, edits, and output settings. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code run Gemini image generation through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the infsh CLI.
Generates images from text prompts with configurable aspect ratio.
Edits input images using prompt instructions and image URLs.
Requests multiple generated images in one run.
Can enable Google Search grounding for current context.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and a working login.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external services.
Quality and availability depend on the remote model and platform.
The skill documents commands; it does not include local image generation code.

use_cases.title: Create campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Generate several concept images with consistent prompt structure, aspect ratio, and resolution.

use_cases.title: Edit reference images
use_cases.description: Apply targeted visual changes to an existing image using natural language instructions.

use_cases.title: Prototype product imagery
use_cases.description: Test composition, lighting, and style directions before commissioning final assets.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana-2 to generate one square image of a modern desk setup with natural morning light.

prompt_templates.title: Styled concept set
prompt_templates.scenario: Create multiple options with a defined style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four 16:9 concept images for a travel banner. Use a clean editorial style, warm light, and realistic details.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted image edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify an existing image while preserving the main subject.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this product image by changing the background to a bright studio setting. Keep the product shape and label unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Grounded visual brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Use current context for a more specific visual result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 4K image that visualizes current Tokyo weather as an elegant city scene. Enable Google Search grounding.

output_examples.input: A request for four minimalist logo concepts for a coffee shop.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the image model and returns multiple generated logo options with a short description.

output_examples.input: A landscape image edit request asking to add a rainbow in the sky.
output_examples.output: The assistant sends the source image and prompt to the service, then returns the edited image result.

output_examples.input: A request for a 16:9 panoramic mountain scene in 4K.
output_examples.output: The assistant generates a high-resolution landscape image using the requested aspect ratio and visual style.

best_practices: Use clear prompts that specify subject, style, composition, lighting, and output size.
Review privacy requirements before sending proprietary images or sensitive prompts to external services.
Generate several lower-resolution drafts before requesting a final 4K result.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, credentials, or confidential customer data into prompts.
Do not assume generated images are licensed for every commercial use without review.
Do not use vague prompts when the output needs strict brand or product accuracy.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant run Gemini image generation and editing through the inference.sh CLI.

faq.question: Does it require an external account?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires the inference.sh CLI and a successful login.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented input supports image references for editing workflows.

faq.question: Does it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. It uses remote inference and documentation services.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and images are sent to external services, so private data needs review first.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs external commands and network services. No confirmed malicious behavior was found.
